Route builder — order, dates, book

Pick your start date, tick places and set nights for each — check-in and check-out dates are computed by chaining from the start date. Edit the # number to reorder: type 2 to slot a place into position 2, everything after shifts down and keeps its nights. The trip always ends back at Las Vegas. The trip map above redraws your route as you go.

#PlaceNightsCheck-inCheck-outKmHWeatherBook

Km are driving distances between consecutive stops; "~" legs are estimates. Hours assume ~95 km/h trip average incl. short breaks. Rows marked alternative are researched options near the route — hover for why, tick to swap them in. Weather is the stay's high/low from Open-Meteo; "≈" means last year's same dates (too far out for a forecast). Booking/Airbnb links open pre-filled searches — availability and prices are theirs.

The places — profiles

Every stop in one gallery. Places on your route in the builder above float to the top, numbered in visit order. Ticket links go to official operators.

Boulder City

stop · US

Step back into the 1930s in this clean, green oasis built for Hoover Dam workers, offering a rare gambling-free Nevada experience rich in Art Deco charm and mid-century Americana.

Stay: €100-160/n historic downtown — cheaper near highway €70-100

Try: Pit-smoked beef brisket, gourmet craft burgers, local Nevada pale ale

  • Hoover Dam Powerplant Tour ~’15 · Powerplant tickets can be bought online; the full Dam Tour is walk-up only on a first-come, first-served basis.
  • Nevada Southern Railway Ride ~’10 · Operates primarily on weekends; book online to secure departure times.
  • Where to eat
  • The Dillinger — Gourmet burgers named after famous gangsters served in a historic bank building. Try the 'Baby Face Nelson' burger, ~’15-25/pp.
  • Fox Smokehouse BBQ — Award-winning, championship-style pit BBQ. The moist beef brisket and pulled pork platters are legendary, ~’20-35/pp.
  • Milo's Cellar & Inn — Cozy, rustic wine bar offering excellent steaks, charcuterie boards, and an extensive wine list, ~’30-50/pp.
  • In & around
  • Hoover Dam — 15 min drive, walk across the massive concrete bypass bridge for incredible views
  • Lake Mead National Recreation Area — 10 min drive, perfect for boating, swimming, and scenic desert drives
  • Valley of Fire State Park — 1 hour drive, famous for its vibrant red Aztec sandstone formations and ancient petoglyphs
  • Notes — read before you go
  • Boulder City is one of only two cities in Nevada that completely bans gambling; do not expect to find slot machines here.
  • Security checkpoints are active on the road to Hoover Dam; expect vehicle inspections and leave firearms or marijuana at home.
  • Summer heat is extreme, often exceeding 40°C (104°F); always carry ample water and hike only in the early morning.
  • Interesting facts
  • The city was entirely master-planned in 1931 by federal architect Saco Rienk DeBoer to house Hoover Dam construction workers.
  • It was established as a federal reservation, meaning residents could not vote or own land until the government relinquished control in 1960.

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · 🍴 where to eat · sight — click a pin for details

More photos on Google Images →

Meadview

stop · US

As Arizona's only community inside the Lake Mead National Recreation Area, Meadview offers a rugged, off-the-beaten-path desert escape with dramatic views of the Grand Canyon's West Rim.

Stay: €90-150/n local cabins & vacation rentals — cheaper RV parks €40-70/n

Try: Mesquite-grilled ribeye steak, classic American cheeseburger, local craft beers

  • Grand Canyon West Rim & Skywalk ~€60 · Pre-booking online is highly recommended to skip the ticket window lines.
  • Lake Mead National Recreation Area Vehicle Pass ~€25 · Required for vehicle entry; valid for 7 consecutive days.
  • Where to eat
  • Meadview Grill — Juicy, classic American burgers and crinkle-cut fries in a casual, welcoming diner, ~€15-25/pp.
  • The Boathouse Bar & Grill — Hearty pub food, cold beers, and local desert hospitality with occasional live music, ~€20-35/pp.
  • In & around
  • Grand Canyon West & Skywalk — 45 min drive, walk on a glass bridge suspended 4,000 feet above the canyon floor
  • South Cove at Lake Mead — 15 min drive, perfect spot for launching a kayak, swimming, or viewing the rugged shoreline
  • Grapevine Mesa Joshua Tree Forest — 20 min drive, drive through one of the densest and oldest Joshua tree stands in Arizona
  • Notes — read before you go
  • Cell service is extremely spotty; download offline Google Maps of the entire area before you depart.
  • Meadview has no major supermarkets; buy your groceries and road-trip snacks in Kingman or Las Vegas.
  • Gas stations are highly limited in this remote area; fill up your tank before driving in.
  • Interesting facts
  • Meadview is the only town in Arizona located entirely within the boundaries of the Lake Mead National Recreation Area.
  • The surrounding Grapevine Mesa is home to a unique, federally protected forest of giant Joshua trees.

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · 🍴 where to eat · sight — click a pin for details

More photos on Google Images →

Seligman

stop · US

The vibrant birthplace of Historic Route 66, Seligman is a neon-lit, retro time capsule packed with vintage Americana, quirky roadside attractions, and classic mid-century diner culture.

Stay: $80-130/n historic motels on Route 66 — cheaper in Kingman $60-90

Try: Route 66 burgers, thick milkshakes, smoked brisket, homemade cherry pie

  • Grand Canyon Caverns Tour ~$25 · Walk-ins are welcome, but advance booking is recommended for the deeper explorer or ghost tours.
  • Grand Canyon National Park Vehicle Pass ~$35 · Valid for 7 days; buy online in advance to bypass the physical payment lanes at the South Entrance.
  • Where to eat
  • Delgadillo's Snow Cap — Famous quirky diner serving classic cheeseburgers, fries, and thick malts with a side of playful jokes from the staff (~$10-15/pp).
  • Roadkill Cafe / OK Saloon — Taxidermy-filled western saloon offering hearty ribeye steaks, buffalo burgers, and BBQ ribs (~$20-35/pp).
  • Westside Lilo's Cafe — Local favorite serving massive chicken fried steaks, authentic German schnitzel, and famous homemade carrot cake (~$15-25/pp).
  • In & around
  • Grand Canyon Caverns — 25 min drive, explore underground limestone caves 200 feet below the surface
  • Hackberry General Store — 50 min drive, iconic vintage Route 66 stop packed with old gas pumps and classic cars
  • Grand Canyon National Park (South Rim) — 1 hr 15 min drive, world-famous scenic overlooks and hiking trails
  • Notes — read before you go
  • Many local shops and diners close early (between 5:00 PM and 7:00 PM), so plan your dinners accordingly.
  • Keep your gas tank full; services can be extremely sparse on the long stretches of Route 66 heading west.
  • Watch out for free-roaming cattle and wildlife like elk, especially when driving at dawn, dusk, or night.
  • Interesting facts
  • Seligman was the primary inspiration for the fictional town of Radiator Springs in Pixar's animated movie 'Cars'.
  • Local barber Angel Delgadillo founded the Historic Route 66 Association here, earning Seligman the title 'Birthplace of Historic Route 66'.

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · 🍴 where to eat · sight — click a pin for details

More photos on Google Images →

Williams

stop · US

Known as the Gateway to the Grand Canyon, Williams is a neon-lit slice of Americana preserved along historic Route 66, offering vintage diner vibes, steam trains, and rugged pine forests.

Stay: €110-180/n historic Route 66 motels — cheaper chain hotels near I-40 €70-110

Try: Smoked brisket, classic Route 66 burgers, craft beer from local microbreweries, Arizona ribeye steak

  • Grand Canyon Railway ~€85 · Book well in advance, especially for first-class parlor cars or during peak summer months.
  • Bearizona Wildlife Park ~€35 · Drive-through park; visit early in the morning when the bears and wolves are most active.
  • Where to eat
  • Rod's Steak House — Charbroiled prime rib or T-bone steak in a historic 1946 Route 66 landmark, ~€30-50/pp.
  • Cruisers Cafe 66 — Slow-smoked baby back ribs and classic burgers served in a retro gas station-themed diner, ~€15-30/pp.
  • Pine Country Restaurant — Hearty chicken fried steak followed by a slice of their famous homemade pies, ~€15-25/pp.
  • In & around
  • Grand Canyon National Park (South Rim) — 60 min drive, iconic vistas and hiking trails.
  • Flagstaff — 35 min drive, historic mountain town with craft breweries and Lowell Observatory.
  • Sycamore Canyon — 45 min drive, rugged wilderness perfect for hiking without the national park crowds.
  • Notes — read before you go
  • Temperatures drop rapidly after sunset due to the high elevation (2,000m), so pack warm layers even in summer.
  • Parking along historic Route 66 is usually free but can be highly competitive during summer afternoons.
  • If taking the Grand Canyon Railway, note that you will only have about 3 hours at the canyon before the return trip.
  • Interesting facts
  • Williams was the very last town on historic Route 66 to be bypassed by Interstate 40, in October 1984.
  • The town is named after William 'Old Bill' Williams, a famous 19th-century mountain man, trapper, and guide.

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · 🍴 where to eat · sight — click a pin for details

More photos on Google Images →

Grand Canyon Village

stop · US

Perched directly on the South Rim, Grand Canyon Village offers immediate access to jaw-dropping canyon vistas, historic rustic architecture, and legendary sunset viewpoints.

Stay: €180-350/n inside the park (historic lodges) — cheaper in Tusayan €120-200/n

Try: Arizona ribeye steak, prickly pear margaritas, Navajo fry bread, smoked bison burgers

  • Grand Canyon National Park Vehicle Pass ~€32 · Valid for 7 days; buy online in advance to speed up entry at the park gates.
  • Grand Canyon Railway ~€80 · Historic western-themed train ride from Williams; book months in advance.
  • Where to eat
  • El Tovar Dining Room — Historic fine dining on the rim; try the hand-cut Arizona Tomahawk Ribeye or bison tenderloin. ~€45-80/pp
  • Big E Steakhouse & Saloon — Western-themed spot in nearby Tusayan serving up hearty ribeye steaks, BBQ ribs, and half-pound burgers. ~€25-45/pp
  • Yavapai Tavern — Casual outdoor patio in the village serving local craft beers, elk burgers, and street tacos. ~€15-25/pp
  • In & around
  • Desert View Watchtower — 35 min drive, iconic Mary Colter-designed stone tower with panoramic canyon views.
  • Bearizona Wildlife Park — 1 hour drive, drive-through wildlife park in Williams featuring black bears and wolves.
  • Horseshoe Bend — 2.5 hour drive, stunning carved sandstone river bend near Page.
  • Notes — read before you go
  • Parking lots near the Visitor Center fill up by 9:00 AM; park early and use the free park shuttle buses to get around.
  • Summer temperatures at the canyon floor can exceed 40°C (104°F); always carry at least 1 liter of water per hour of hiking.
  • Wildlife like elk and mule deer roam freely in the village; maintain a safe distance of at least 25 yards.
  • Interesting facts
  • The Grand Canyon is so massive that it creates its own microclimates and weather patterns.
  • Grand Canyon Village was specifically designed by the Santa Fe Railroad in the early 1900s to boost luxury tourism.

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · 🍴 where to eat · sight — click a pin for details

More photos on Google Images →

Page

stop · US

Carved into the Navajo sandstone, Page is your gateway to the swirling orange corridors of Antelope Canyon and the dizzying, horseshoe-shaped plunge of the Colorado River.

Stay: €120-220/n near Lake Powell Blvd — cheaper in winter €70-110

Try: Navajo taco, smoked beef brisket, prickly pear margarita

  • Antelope Canyon Guided Tour ~€60-100 · Mandatory guided tour; book 3-6 months in advance as walk-ins are not permitted.
  • Horseshoe Bend Parking ~€9 · Paid parking fee per vehicle on-site; no advance reservations but sunset gets highly congested.
  • Where to eat
  • Big John's Texas BBQ — Slow-smoked beef brisket and baby back ribs served on picnic tables in a converted gas station, ~€20-35/pp.
  • State 48 Tavern — Gourmet burgers, local Arizona craft beers, and pub favorites in a lively local setting, ~€15-25/pp.
  • Slackers — No-nonsense, thick-cut burgers and hand-spun milkshakes perfect after a day of hiking, ~€12-20/pp.
  • In & around
  • Glen Canyon Dam & Lake Powell — 10 min drive, massive concrete arch dam with scenic lake overlooks and boat rentals.
  • Monument Valley — 2 hour drive, iconic towering sandstone buttes made famous by classic Hollywood Westerns.
  • Toadstool Hoodoos — 30 min drive, easy 1.5-mile hike to bizarre mushroom-shaped rock formations.
  • Notes — read before you go
  • Be aware of timezone confusion: Page (Arizona) does not observe Daylight Saving Time, but the nearby Navajo Nation does.
  • Bring plenty of water and wear sturdy shoes; even short walks like Horseshoe Bend have no shade and intense desert heat.
  • Flash floods are a serious danger in slot canyons; tours will be canceled instantly if rain is forecast anywhere nearby.
  • Interesting facts
  • Page was established in 1957 as a temporary coffer-dam construction camp for workers building the Glen Canyon Dam.
  • The town's land was acquired through a land exchange with the Navajo Nation in exchange for ranch land in Utah.

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · 🍴 where to eat · sight — click a pin for details

More photos on Google Images →

Springdale

stop · US

Gateway to the towering red sandstone cliffs of Zion National Park, Springdale offers a perfect base for exploring iconic American canyon country with a vibrant local food scene.

Stay: €180-350/n near park entrance — cheaper in Hurricane €90-150/n

Try: Utah-style smoked brisket, local craft beers, Navajo tacos

  • Angels Landing Permit ~€6 · Seasonal lottery application required months in advance, or day-before lottery.
  • Zion National Park Vehicle Pass ~€32 · Valid for 7 days; America the Beautiful annual pass accepted.
  • Where to eat
  • Bit & Spur Restaurant & Saloon — Sweet chili glazed ribs and ribeye steak — ~€25-45/pp.
  • Oscar's Cafe — Garlic burger and massive Southwestern plates — ~€15-25/pp.
  • Whiptail Grill — Spiced goat cheese burger and avocado pupusas in a converted gas station — ~€15-30/pp.
  • In & around
  • Grafton Ghost Town — 20 min drive, iconic preserved 19th-century Mormon pioneer settlement.
  • Kolob Canyons — 50 min drive, quieter red-rock canyons with scenic viewpoints.
  • Sand Hollow State Park — 40 min drive, warm reservoir with red sand beaches and ATV dunes.
  • Notes — read before you go
  • Zion Canyon Scenic Drive is closed to private vehicles most of the year; you must use the free park shuttle.
  • Parking inside Zion fills up by 7:00 AM; park in Springdale and take the free town shuttle to the pedestrian entrance.
  • Flash floods are a serious danger in slot canyons like The Narrows; always check the weather forecast and flow rate before entering.
  • Interesting facts
  • Springdale was settled in 1862 by Mormon pioneers who established farms along the Virgin River.
  • The town is completely surrounded by the towering cliffs of Zion National Park, offering 360-degree canyon views from any street.

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · 🍴 where to eat · sight — click a pin for details

More photos on Google Images →

Mammoth Lakes

stop · US

A dramatic alpine basin cradled by the Sierra Nevada, offering access to otherworldly basalt columns, pristine glacial lakes, and classic mountain-town hospitality.

Stay: $150-250/n near the Village — cheaper along Old Mammoth Rd $100-150/n

Try: Tri-tip BBQ, Mammoth Brewing craft beer, locally caught trout

  • Devils Postpile Shuttle Ticket ~$15 · Mandatory shuttle access during peak summer operating hours.
  • Yosemite National Park Entry ~$35 · Required if planning to enter Yosemite via the nearby Tioga Pass during peak season.
  • Where to eat
  • Burgers Restaurant — Thick, juicy charbroiled burgers and curly fries in a casual, rustic lodge (~$15-25/pp).
  • The Mogul Restaurant — Classic mountain steakhouse known for prime rib and charbroiled steaks (~$40-60/pp).
  • In & around
  • Bodie State Historic Park — 1 hour drive, a remarkably preserved, authentic gold-mining ghost town.
  • Mono Lake Tufa State Natural Reserve — 30 min drive, eerie limestone towers rising from an ancient saline lake.
  • June Lake Loop — 25 min drive, scenic drive past four stunning alpine lakes with mountain backdrops.
  • Notes — read before you go
  • Altitude sickness is common; the town sits at 7,800 feet (2,400m), so stay hydrated and limit alcohol on day one.
  • Bear-safe food storage is strictly enforced; never leave food, trash, or scented items in your car.
  • Tioga Pass (Yosemite access) and Reds Meadow Road close seasonally due to heavy winter snow, often until late May or June.
  • Interesting facts
  • Mammoth Mountain is actually a dormant lava dome complex that last erupted about 57,000 years ago.
  • The area's extreme high altitude makes it a premier training ground for US Olympic long-distance runners.

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · 🍴 where to eat · sight — click a pin for details

More photos on Google Images →

Yosemite Valley

stop · US

Witness towering granite monoliths like El Capitan and Half Dome framing a glacier-carved valley floor of roaring waterfalls and ancient giant sequoias.

Stay: €250-450/n inside the valley — cheaper outside in Mariposa or Oakhurst €120-200/n

Try: Bison burgers, tri-tip BBQ, boysenberry pie, local Sierra Nevada craft beers

  • Yosemite National Park Entrance Pass ~€33 per vehicle · Peak hours reservation required on select dates in spring and summer; pass is valid for 3 consecutive days.
  • Half Dome Cables Permit ~€9 · Required to hike to the top of Half Dome; awarded via pre-season lottery in March and daily lotteries during hiking season.
  • Where to eat
  • The Ahwahnee Dining Room — Prime rib and dry-aged steaks served in a historic, high-ceilinged stone and timber dining hall — ~€60-100/pp
  • Base Camp Eatery — Hearty classic burgers, fries, and local draft beers in a casual lodge setting — ~€15-25/pp
  • South Gate Brewing Company (Oakhurst) — House-smoked tri-tip, gourmet burgers, and award-winning craft beers just outside the park — ~€25-45/pp
  • In & around
  • Glacier Point — 1 hour drive, offering breathtaking panoramic views of Yosemite Valley and the High Sierra.
  • Mariposa Grove — 1 hour drive, home to over 500 mature giant sequoias including the Grizzly Giant.
  • Tuolumne Meadows — 1.5 hours drive, a pristine subalpine meadow along the scenic Tioga Road.
  • Notes — read before you go
  • Bear-proof food lockers must be used; never leave food, drinks, or scented toiletries in your vehicle as bears will break in.
  • Yosemite Valley parking lots routinely fill up by 9:00 AM during peak season; park early and use the free shuttle bus.
  • Download offline maps and travel guides before entering the park, as cellular service is extremely weak or non-existent.
  • Interesting facts
  • Yosemite was central to the birth of the national park idea, first protected by Abraham Lincoln signing the Yosemite Grant in 1864.
  • El Capitan is one of the world's premier rock-climbing destinations, rising over 3,000 feet (900 meters) sheer from the valley floor.

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · 🍴 where to eat · sight — click a pin for details

More photos on Google Images →

Lone Pine

stop · US

Nestled at the foot of Mount Whitney, Lone Pine is a legendary Sierra Nevada stopover famous for the Alabama Hills, a surreal rock landscape that has starred in hundreds of classic Hollywood Westerns.

Stay: $110-180/n town center — budget motels along US-395 $80-110

Try: Tri-tip steak, classic Western burgers, Sierra Nevada craft beer

  • Museum of Western Film History ~$10 · Pay at the door, but check seasonal winter hours before visiting.
  • Where to eat
  • Seasons Restaurant — High-quality steaks, local pan-seared trout, and an excellent wine list in a cozy mountain setting. ~$30-50/pp
  • Alabama Hills Cafe & Bakery — Hearty American breakfasts, massive biscuits and gravy, and classic burgers. ~$15-25/pp
  • In & around
  • Alabama Hills — 10 min drive, iconic rounded rock formations, movie filming locations, and the Mobius Arch trail
  • Manzanar National Historic Site — 15 min drive, highly moving and educational preserved WWII Japanese-American internment camp
  • Whitney Portal — 20 min drive, dramatic winding road leading to the trailhead of the highest peak in the contiguous US
  • Notes — read before you go
  • Summer temperatures in the Alabama Hills regularly exceed 100°F (38°C); carry plenty of extra water and avoid hiking midday.
  • Gas prices along the US-395 corridor are notoriously high; fill up your tank in larger towns before arriving.
  • If planning to camp in the Alabama Hills, note that dispersed camping is now strictly limited to designated sites to protect the fragile desert.
  • Interesting facts
  • More than 400 films and television shows have been shot in the Alabama Hills, including 'Iron Man', 'Gladiator', and 'Django Unchained'.
  • Lone Pine sits in the shadow of Mount Whitney (14,505 ft), the highest peak in the contiguous US, yet is only 100 miles from Badwater Basin, the lowest point.

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · 🍴 where to eat · sight — click a pin for details
